title: Project scaffold — Expo app with navigation and Firebase
description: Init Expo project, install dependencies (Firebase, Zustand, React Navigation), configure EXPO_PUBLIC_* env vars, set up bottom tab navigator with placeholder screens, configure Firebase auth and Firestore connection.

title: Auth screens — Register and Login
description: Build RegisterScreen and LoginScreen using Firebase Auth email/password. Reuse TapIn patterns: form validation, toast errors, redirect to app on success. Store user profile (uid, displayName, email) in Firestore /users/{uid}.

title: Auth store — useAuthStore with Zustand
description: Zustand store that listens to Firebase onAuthStateChanged, loads user profile from Firestore, exposes user, loading, signOut. Same pattern as TapIn useAuth.

title: Group create and join flow
description: JoinOrCreateGroupScreen shown to users with no group. Two paths: (1) Create group — generates alphanumeric inviteCode, writes /groups/{id} doc, sets user as admin. (2) Join group — enter invite code, look up group, add user as member. On success navigate to main tabs.

title: Group Firestore service
description: groupService.ts: createGroup(name) → writes doc, generates inviteCode. joinGroup(inviteCode) → queries by inviteCode, adds member. leaveGroup(groupId). getGroup(groupId). useGroupStore Zustand store.

title: Technique library screen
description: LibraryScreen: list all techniques in the group sorted by createdAt desc. Each card shows title, category, value rating badge (Meme Bullshido / Situationally Nasty / High Percentage God Tier), and video indicator if videoUrl set. Tap to open TechniqueDetailScreen.

title: Technique Firestore service and store
description: techniqueService.ts: addTechnique, updateTechnique, deleteTechnique, subscribeTechniques (realtime listener). useTechniqueStore Zustand store. Types: Technique with id, title, category, position, notes, videoUrl, value(0|1|2|null), addedBy, addedByName, createdAt, tags.

title: Add and edit technique screens
description: CreateTechniqueScreen: form for title (required), category (picker), position (free text), notes, videoUrl, value rating selector. EditTechniqueScreen: same form pre-filled. Delete with ConfirmModal. No Alert.alert — use ConfirmModal + toast.

title: Open mat queue screen
description: QueueScreen: list queue items for the group. Each item shows technique title, who added it, drillGrade badge (null=not drilled yet / Struggled / Getting It / Nailed It). Tap to grade. Button to add technique from library to queue. Button to clear drilled items.

title: Queue Firestore service and store
description: queueService.ts: addToQueue(techniqueId), gradeQueueItem(queueItemId, grade: 0|1|2), removeFromQueue, clearDrilled, subscribeQueue. useQueueStore Zustand store. QueueItem type: id, techniqueId, techniqueTitle, addedBy, addedByName, addedAt, sessionLabel, drillGrade(0|1|2|null).

title: Technique detail screen
description: TechniqueDetailScreen: shows all technique fields. If videoUrl set, show tappable link that opens in browser (Linking.openURL). Add to Queue button. Edit button (author or admin only). Value rating display with correct label.

title: Profile screen
description: ProfileScreen: show display name, email, group name, invite code (tap to copy). Leave group (ConfirmModal). Sign out (ConfirmModal). Admin sees regenerate invite code button.

title: Firestore security rules
description: Write security rules: authenticated users can only read/write their own group's subcollections. Only group members can read techniques and queue. Only admin can delete group. Document in FIRESTORE_RULES.md.

title: Shared components — ConfirmModal, Toast, theme
description: Port ConfirmModal from TapIn. Set up toast utility. Define theme (colors, spacing, typography) consistent with a BJJ app aesthetic. These are prerequisites for all UI tasks.

title: Design Google Sign-In strategy for Expo managed workflow
description: Decide auth approach: expo-auth-session + expo-web-browser for native OAuth flow, signInWithPopup for web. Document what needs enabling in Firebase console (Google provider, OAuth client IDs, authorised redirect URIs). Define how Google auth integrates with existing email/password flow and user profile creation.

title: Implement Google Sign-In in authService
description: Install expo-auth-session and expo-web-browser. Add signInWithGoogle() to authService.ts: on web use signInWithPopup(GoogleAuthProvider), on native use expo-auth-session OAuth flow then signInWithCredential. Create /users/{uid} doc on first Google sign-in using displayName and email from Google profile. No password field.

title: Add Google Sign-In button to LoginScreen and RegisterScreen
description: Add a Google sign-in button (Google logo + 'Continue with Google') to both LoginScreen and RegisterScreen. Calls signInWithGoogle from authService. Shows loading state. On success auth store picks up the user and navigates automatically. On error shows toast.
